title: Recon Automator
learning_objectives: Build a functional Python recon automation script
Implement input validation, WHOIS lookups, and DNS resolution
scenario: Your team performs the same passive recon steps on every engagement. It is time to automate. Build a Python script that takes a domain as input and automates basic OSINT lookups, saving everything to a timestamped report.

scenario_context.challenge: Build a working Python recon tool from scratch
scenario_context.relevance: Automation is what separates a script kiddie from a professional operator. Building your own tools develops deep understanding.

subtasks.title: Domain Input Handler
subtasks.description: Create a script that accepts a domain, validates the format, and stores it for processing.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use sys.argv or argparse for input. Validate with regex or simple checks. Handle missing input gracefully with usage message.

subtasks.title: WHOIS Lookup Function
subtasks.description: Add a function that performs a WHOIS lookup and extracts key fields (registrar, creation date, expiry).
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use python-whois library. Extract registrar, creation_date, expiration_date, name_servers. Handle lookup failures with try/except.
subtasks.evidence_required: Script output showing parsed WHOIS data

subtasks.title: DNS Resolution Function
subtasks.description: Add a function that resolves the domain to IPs and performs reverse DNS lookups.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use socket.gethostbyname() and socket.gethostbyaddr(). Handle resolution failures. Try multiple record types if possible.

subtasks.title: Report Generator
subtasks.description: Add output formatting that saves all results to a timestamped text file.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Create output directory. Generate filename with timestamp. Write results with clear headers and formatting. Include run metadata.
subtasks.evidence_required: Generated report file and complete script source

reflection_prompt: Your script works perfectly in the lab but fails on a real engagement because DNS is blocked. How do you build resilience into your tools?

quiz.question: try/except in Python network scripts is important because:
options: Network operations frequently fail and unhandled exceptions crash the script
quiz.explanation: Network operations are unreliable. DNS lookups fail, connections time out, and services refuse connections. Error handling keeps the script running.

quiz.question: Timestamped output files are important because:
options: They prevent overwriting previous results and provide an audit trail
quiz.explanation: Timestamps ensure each engagement's results are preserved separately, creating an audit trail for reporting and legal purposes.

mentor_notes: Code must run. Test by executing the submitted script against a test domain. Evaluate: does it handle errors? Is the output useful? Is the code readable?
